rationale: The citing paper uses Kashyap, Antia, and Frazier (2012) to support a specific theoretical claim: isolated disciplinary efforts are ineffective. This claim is part of the core deterrence framing used to justify the bench program as a multifaceted threat and to motivate the hypotheses. The citation is therefore more than a passing background mention, even though the paper does not directly reuse the cited study's data or model.
relation_description: Boichuk et al. draw on Kashyap et al.'s finding about the limits of isolated discipline to justify their intervention design and conceptual setup around effective deterrence. The cited paper functions as prior evidence underpinning why a multifaceted punishment threat should matter.
